title: Page-level content: public read-only render
description: Public-side rendering of page body content: the logged-out documentation view must show a page's body, with tabs (if any) as additional views. Builds on the data model (TASK-65), pipeline (TASK-66), and admin UX (TASK-67).
acceptanceCriteria: The public page render (`/docs/:sectionSlug/:pageSlug`) shows the published page body content
When a page has user tabs, they render as additional views alongside/after the body; when it has none, only the body shows (no empty tab bar)
Existing published pages still render correctly after the TASK-65 migration (no regression for tab-only pages)
Search results and public navigation link to the page body correctly; the primary/body container is not exposed as a separate tab URL to visitors

steps.details: Update `apps/admin/src/features/public/PublicPageView.tsx` and `PageRenderer.tsx` to render the primary/body container from the latest published revision as the page body.

When user tabs exist, render them as additional views (reuse the existing public tab rendering). Ensure the primary container isn't shown as a user tab and its tabId isn't used as a public URL segment.

With a published page that has body-only content, confirm `/docs/...` renders it logged out; add a tab, republish, confirm body + tab both render; confirm a pre-existing tab-only page still renders. Confirm search deep-links land on the right content.

technicalNotes: `PageRenderer` is shared between the admin revision preview and the public view — changes here also affect the read-only revision preview; verify both.
The public route already handles `/docs/:sectionSlug/:pageSlug` and `/docs/:sectionSlug/:pageSlug/:tabId`; decide how the body maps into that scheme without breaking existing tab deep-links.
